sprint_meta.notes: WK20 — UI Input Bridge & Camera Polish (Agent 12 Tools/DevEx reply, Round 1 spec)
WK20 Round 1 — Agent 12 (Tools/DevEx): debug mechanism spec for verifying Ursina→Pygame mouse mapping and (later) floor raycast — specs only, no code this round

round_meta.build_target: Specs & contracts — no implementation this round

wk20_r1_kickoff.prompt_text: WK20 Round 1 (UI Input Bridge & Camera Polish): Agent 12 — propose a lightweight debug mechanism (print on click and/or on-screen debug text) to verify mouse translation works before full hooks in Round 2. Forget prior logging instructions.

response.raw:
Agent 12 (ToolsDevEx_Lead) — Round 1 deliverable is a concrete debug contract for WK20, not implementation.

Problem: We need to prove, with minimal surface area, that (A) Ursina-reported pointer positions map to the same pixel space as `engine.screen` after Agent 08’s math, and (B) once Agent 03’s raycast exists, the same click produces a stable world/floor sample (and eventually tile indices) without guessing from logs alone.

Recommended approach — two complementary channels, both gated behind a single explicit “input debug” switch so normal play and CI stay quiet:

1) **On-click console line (primary)**  
- Trigger: only on mouse button down (e.g. LMB), not every frame.  
- One compact UTF-8 line to stdout (or to a dedicated logger at INFO) with fixed field order, e.g.:  
  `WK20_INPUT raw_ursina=(sx,sy) pygame=(px,py) ui_hit={0|1} world_xz=(wx,wz|na) tile=(tx,ty|na)`  
- `ui_hit` is optional in R1 spec but should be reserved for Agent 08 once HUD hit-test exists: 1 = click consumed by Pygame overlay, 0 = passed through to world.  
- `world_xz` / `tile` may print `na` until Agent 03 wires raycast → sim contract; Round 2 fills them in without changing the log format.  
- Implementation location (Round 2): centralize in the Ursina input path (e.g. `UrsinaInputManager` / `ursina_app` frame handler) so there is a single source of truth — not scattered prints in gameplay code.

2) **Optional on-screen debug strip (secondary)**  
- Small read-only text block parented to `camera.ui` (same stack as Pygame overlay per WK19 notes), top-left or bottom-left, 3–5 lines: last raw mouse, last mapped pygame coords, last world XZ / tile if available, and `ui_hit` if available.  
- Updates only on pointer move or click (throttled move to e.g. 10 Hz if needed) to avoid per-frame text churn and perf noise.  
- Non-interactive: must not intercept mouse events (z behind interactive HUD or purely visual).

Activation contract:  
- **Config flag** (e.g. in existing settings or `config.py` dev section) **or** environment variable e.g. `KINGDOM_URSINA_DEBUG_INPUT=1` — Tools prefers env + one code path so headless/CI can leave it unset. Default **off**.  
- Document the flag in a single bullet in `tools/` or engine README slice if PM wants; Agent 12 can add a one-line `--help` or internal doc reference in Round 2.

Scope boundaries:  
- This debug layer is **telemetry only** — no writes to simulation state, no LLM taps, no persistence.  
- Does not replace QA smoke; optional manual verification aid for Ursina windowed runs on Windows.  
- Coordinate with Agent 11: if any automated test ever runs Ursina with this flag, assert log volume stays bounded (click-only, not per-frame spam).

Forget prior “PM logging instructions” for this sprint: this spec supersedes generic agent log verbosity for WK20 input debugging.
summary_bullets: Define a single gated “input debug” switch (env or config), default off, for Ursina mouse/raycast verification.
Primary: one-line structured stdout (or logger) on mouse down with raw Ursina coords, mapped pygame pixels, reserved ui_hit, and optional world_xz/tile (na until wired).
Secondary: optional camera.ui read-only text strip showing last values; update on click or throttled move; non-interactive.
Centralize emission in the Ursina input bridge (Round 2) — not in sim systems.
Telemetry only; no sim state side effects; CI/headless remain quiet unless flag is explicitly set.
proposed_changes: Round 2 (implementation): Add env/config gate `KINGDOM_URSINA_DEBUG_INPUT` (or equivalent) read once at Ursina client init.
Round 2: Implement click-time single-line log in `UrsinaInputManager` / `ursina_app` after Agent 08 exposes pygame mapping and Agent 03 exposes raycast → (wx,wz) / tile.
Round 2: Optional `camera.ui` Text entity for last-known coords; throttle pointer-move updates; document z-order so it does not block HUD.
Round 2: Add one short subsection to QA/dev docs (or `QA_TEST_PLAN.md` Ursina manual section) listing the flag and expected log line format.
Round 2: If `tools/qa_smoke.py` ever launches Ursina, ensure debug flag is off by default in that profile.
acceptance_criteria: With debug **disabled** (default): zero extra input-related prints and no new on-screen debug widgets; behavior unchanged from pre-WK20 baseline aside from future functional fixes.

With debug **enabled**: every LMB (or agreed primary button) down emits **exactly one** structured line containing at minimum `raw_ursina` and `pygame` pixel coordinates once Agent 08’s mapping is integrated; line format is stable enough for copy-paste diff in manual testing.

With debug enabled and raycast integrated: the same line includes non-`na` `world_xz` and `tile` when the click misses UI and hits the floor per Agent 03’s contract (or documents why `na` e.g. miss sky).
Optional on-screen strip: when enabled, shows the same logical values as the last log line (within one frame); does not steal focus or block Pygame UI hit testing.
No per-frame unconditional stdout spam; pointer-move logging either off or throttled.
Debug path does not call into sim authoritatively (read-only inspection of coordinates only).

Manual verification recipe for Round 2 signoff: enable flag → click a known HUD button → expect ui_hit=1 and stable pygame coords matching surface tests; click world → expect ui_hit=0 and tile coords moving with camera per Agent 03.

risks.description: Stdout noise: if throttling is wrong or another system prints every frame, logs become unusable and hide real errors.
risks.mitigation: Click-only primary path; cap move logs; document “quiet unless flag” for CI.

risks.description: Coordinate space confusion: mixing window pixels, Ursina normalized coords, and pygame surface size could make debug lines misleading.
risks.mitigation: Field names fixed in contract; Agent 08/03 review log line before merge; test at 1080p and one other resolution if resize exists.

risks.description: On-screen debug Text could obscure HUD or sit above interactive elements if z-order is wrong.
risks.mitigation: Explicit z behind full-screen quad or corner placement; non-interactive; prefer console line as source of truth for Round 2.

risks.description: Headless / no-display runs: Ursina may not run; debug flag should no-op cleanly without crashing.
risks.mitigation: Guard all debug UI creation behind Ursina window active; skip Text if camera.ui unavailable.

risks.description: Determinism: printing wall-clock or noisy floats in sim paths could theoretically affect future tests — debug must stay outside sim tick authoritativeness.
risks.mitigation: Keep telemetry in Ursina/input layer only; never import into `game/systems` for this feature.

dependencies: Agent 08: mathematical mapping from Ursina mouse to `engine.screen` pixels — debug line must use the same function as real UI hit tests.
Agent 03: raycast from screen/camera to XZ floor and contract for tile → sim injection — debug line fills world_xz/tile from the same path as gameplay.
Agent 11 (consult): optional note in manual QA checklist for Ursina input verification using the flag.
questions_back_to_pm: Preferred activation: environment variable only, `config.py` flag only, or both (Tools recommends both with env overriding config for one-off sessions)?
Should the debug line use Python `print()` or the project’s standard logging facility so IDE filters apply consistently?
recommended_next_actions: Agent 08 + 03: agree on field names and order for the one-line log so Round 2 implementation is a single shared format string.
Round 2: Agent 12 (or owning agent per file ownership) implements gate + logging in Ursina input layer; Agent 11 adds a one-step manual check to RELEASE or QA doc.
After first green build: Jaimie runs with flag on, confirms HUD click vs world click show distinct ui_hit / tile values.

agent_fields.wk20_round1_deliverable: Debug mechanism specification (gated console line + optional camera.ui strip); acceptance and risks; no code in Round 1
agent_fields.coordination_notes: Same raycast and pygame mapping used for real input must drive debug output — no duplicate math paths.
